properties.description:
Through commemoration, exhibitions and educational programs, The National September 11 Memorial & Museum, a nonprofit in New York City, remembers and honors the 2,983 people killed in the horrific attacks of September 11, 2001, and February 26, 1993, as well as those who risked their lives to save others and all who demonstrated extraordinary compassion in the aftermath of the attacks.

9/11 Memorial, Open Daily, 7-days, 10 a.m. to 5 p.m.

9/11 Memorial Museum, Open Thursday–Monday, 10 a.m. to 5 p.m.

Thanksgiving and Christmas: Memorial Only, 10 a.m. to 4 p.m.; Museum Closed

properties.description: The American Museum of Natural History is one of the world’s preeminent scientific and cultural institutions. Since its founding in 1869, the Museum has advanced its global mission to discover, interpret, and disseminate information about human cultures, the natural world, and the universe through a wide-ranging program of scientific research, education, and exhibition. The Museum is renowned for its exhibitions and scientific collections, which serve as a field guide to the entire planet and present a panorama of the world's cultures.

properties.description: New York Public Library’s monumental main branch is known for its stunning architecture and remarkable historical collections. Take a break from the city’s hustle and bustle at the Rose Main Reading Room, amid chandeliers and an ornate ceiling with murals. Enjoy the free guided tour of the building and view masterpieces like the first Gutenberg Bible, and bring the kids to see the original stuffed animals that inspired the Winnie the Pooh stories. The library is served by multiple subway lines and is adjacent to the gorgeous Bryant Park, where you can unwind after the visit. – Tripadvisor

properties.description: Gallaghers Steakhouse is an American steakhouse established in New York City in 1927. Located just steps from the dazzling lights of Broadway and the pulsing energy of Times Square, Gallaghers Steakhouse takes its rightful place at the very heart of New York City. For over 87 years, this is where the city's sports teams have announced their wins, where celebrities take their bows, and Wall Street titans toast their deals. With its trademark combination of the best USDA prime dry-aged steak, a classic cocktail list, and a wait staff that never forgets a name, Gallaghers serves up an authentic New York City experience you'll savor for years to come.

properties.description: Piccola Cucina Osteria it's our gem in the heart of SoHo. The Chef and owner Philip Guardione opened in January 2013 to bring in the city the real taste of Sicily. Friendly service with a buzzy atmosphere, all in a typical Italian restaurant setting. It is matched by home cooking like you would expect to eat in Italy, which is one of the keys to the success of the restaurant. Fully renovated in 2021 the location looks warm and cozy. Our other two Piccola Cucina locations in Manhattan are:Piccola Cucina Estiatorio ( 75 Thompson street )Piccola Cucina Uptown ( 106 E 60th Street )Piccola Cucina Osteria accepts online requests for parties smaller than 6 guests only.If you are looking for a bigger party or special event, please contact us info@piccolacucinagroup.comThank you

properties.name: Westway Diner
properties.description: Established in 1988, Westway Diner is a family owned and operated restaurant. Well-known as a local favorite, you will always see a familiar face enjoying the food and the atmosphere, especially the murals alongside one wall that reflect life in the "Big Apple." Westway serves and delivers to all, whether they are residents of the area, business people or tourists. And with an extensive eight-page menu, everybody can be satisfied. Have a craving for a waffle at 10 PM? Don't you worry, because all the breakfast items are available at any time! And that's just the beginning, with sandwiches, salads, desserts and much more available whenever you want them. The choices are endless. What are you in the mood for?

properties.name: Lobster Place
properties.description: Lobster Place is a seafood experience like no other. Inside our Chelsea Market space you'll find a top-notch fresh seafood counter, raw bar, sushi bar, fresh steamed lobsters, lobster rolls and other fine casual fare to stay or to go - not to mention our Michelin-acclaimed Cull & Pistol Oyster Bar. We've been pioneering unique seafood experiences since 1974 when we first brought live lobster to NYC directly from the Maine coast - and a visit to our Chelsea Market outlet will show you the latest unforgettable chapter in that 45+ year story.
